Pain disorders related to psychological.description: Guidance for coding pain disorders with psychological components.
Pain disorders related to psychological.F45.41: Assign for pain exclusively related to psychological disorders. Per Excludes 1 under G89, do not assign a G89 code with F45.41.
Pain disorders related to psychological.F45.42 with G89: Use F45.42 with a code from category G89 (Pain, NEC) when documentation shows a psychological component in a patient with acute or chronic pain.

In Remission.description: Selection of remission codes (e.g., -.11, -.21, -.91 under F10–F19) requires provider clinical judgment and is based on provider documentation unless otherwise instructed.
In Remission.mapping: Mild SUD in early/sustained remission → substance abuse in remission codes; Moderate/severe SUD in early/sustained remission → substance dependence in remission codes.

Psychoactive Substance Use, Abuse and De.description: When documentation mentions multiple patterns for the same substance, assign only one code per the hierarchy.
hierarchy: If both use and abuse are documented → assign abuse only.
If both abuse and dependence are documented → assign dependence only.
If use, abuse, and dependence are all documented → assign dependence only.
If both use and dependence are documented → assign dependence only.

Psychoactive Substance Use, Unspecified.usage: Assign only when they meet the definition of a reportable diagnosis and documentation shows the use is associated with a substance-related disorder (Chapter 5) or a medical condition, with the relationship documented by the provider.
Psychoactive Substance Use, Unspecified.reference: See Section III, Reporting Additional Diagnoses.

Medical Conditions Due to Psychoactive S.classification: Medical conditions due to substance use/abuse/dependence are not substance-induced disorders.
Medical Conditions Due to Psychoactive S.coding: Assign the code for the medical condition (per Alphabetical Index) plus the appropriate F1x use/abuse/dependence code.
Medical Conditions Due to Psychoactive S.example: Alcoholic pancreatitis due to alcohol dependence → K85.2- (Alcohol-induced acute pancreatitis) + F10.20 (Alcohol dependence, uncomplicated); do NOT assign F10.288.

Blood Alcohol Level.Y90: A Y90 code may be assigned when blood alcohol level is documented and the provider has documented a condition classifiable to F10 (Alcohol-related disorders).
Blood Alcohol Level.documentation: Blood alcohol level need not be documented by the patient’s provider.
Blood Alcohol Level.reference: See Section I.B.14. for blood alcohol level documentation by clinicians other than the patient's provider.

Factitious Disorder.description: Coding for factitious disorders (imposed on self and by proxy).
Factitious Disorder.Factitious disorder imposed on self (Mun: Assign appropriate code from F68.1- (Factitious disorder imposed on self).

Factitious disorder imposed on another (.perpetrator: Assign F68.A (Factitious disorder imposed on another) to the perpetrator’s record.
Factitious disorder imposed on another (.victim: Assign appropriate code from T74 (confirmed) or T76 (suspected) for adult/child abuse, neglect, or other maltreatment.
Factitious disorder imposed on another (.reference: See Section I.C.19.f. Adult and child abuse, neglect and other maltreatment.

Dementia.description: Classification and severity coding for dementia.
Dementia.categories: F01, F02, F03 classified by etiology and severity (unspecified, mild, moderate, severe).
Dementia.severity_selection: Requires provider clinical judgment and is assigned based on provider documentation unless otherwise instructed by classification.
Dementia.unspecified_severity: If severity is not documented, assign the appropriate code for unspecified severity.
Dementia.progression_during_stay: If admitted at one severity level and progresses to higher severity in an inpatient setting, assign one code for the highest severity level reported during the stay.
